inputs.label: Crop Type
inputs.businessContext: Crop type determines water requirement and yield response factors. Based on FAO Irrigation and Drainage Paper 66.

inputs.label: Irrigation Method
inputs.businessContext: Irrigation method affects water application efficiency. Typical efficiencies: drip 90%, sprinkler 75%, furrow 60%, flood 50% (FAO).

inputs.label: Water Applied
inputs.businessContext: Total water applied during growing season in mm. Based on field measurements or irrigation scheduling.

inputs.label: Crop Water Requirement
inputs.businessContext: Optimal water needed for full yield potential. Derived from evapotranspiration (ETc) calculations per FAO-56.

inputs.label: Yield Potential
inputs.businessContext: Maximum achievable yield under optimal conditions. Based on local agronomic trials or historical data.

inputs.label: Price per Ton
inputs.businessContext: Market price of the crop. Used to calculate revenue loss.

inputs.label: Water Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per mm of water applied (includes pumping, distribution, etc.).

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data quality. Used for risk adjustment.

thresholds.yieldLossRatio: if (1 - actualYield / yieldPotential) > 0.2 then 'High yield loss'

hiddenLossDrivers: if yieldLoss > 0.2 * yieldPotential then 'Significant yield loss'
suggestedActions: Improve irrigation scheduling to match crop water requirement
Consider switching to more efficient irrigation method
